title: Booking a Dental Filling
summary: A patient calls a dental clinic to book a filling, asking about the appointment, the cost, and what to expect.

body.speaker: Hannah
body.text: Hi, I'd like to book an appointment. I think I need a filling.

body.speaker: Robert
body.text: Of course. Have you been to our clinic before?

body.speaker: Hannah
body.text: Yes, I came for a check-up last year. My name is Hannah Lee.

body.speaker: Robert
body.text: Thank you. What seems to be the problem with your tooth?

body.speaker: Hannah
body.text: One of my back teeth hurts when I drink something cold.

body.speaker: Robert
body.text: That could be a small cavity. The dentist will take an X-ray first to be sure.

body.speaker: Hannah
body.text: I see. How much does a filling usually cost?

body.speaker: Robert
body.text: A simple filling is around ninety dollars, but it depends on the size.

body.speaker: Hannah
body.text: Does my insurance cover part of it?

body.speaker: Robert
body.text: It often does. Please bring your insurance card so we can check.

body.speaker: Robert
body.text: Don't worry. The dentist will numb the area, so you won't feel much pain.

body.speaker: Hannah
body.text: That's a relief. Can I come in this Thursday morning?

body.speaker: Robert
body.text: We have an opening at ten o'clock. I'll book you in for then.

body.speaker: Hannah
body.text: Perfect. Thank you for your help.

body.speaker: Robert
body.text: You're welcome. We'll see you on Thursday at ten.

sentences.speaker: Hannah
sentences.text: Hi, I'd like to book an appointment.
sentences.translationVi: Xin chào, tôi muốn đặt một cuộc hẹn.

sentences.speaker: Hannah
sentences.text: I think I need a filling.
sentences.translationVi: Tôi nghĩ tôi cần trám răng.

sentences.speaker: Robert
sentences.text: Of course.
sentences.translationVi: Tất nhiên rồi.

sentences.speaker: Robert
sentences.text: Have you been to our clinic before?
sentences.translationVi: Bạn đã từng đến phòng khám của chúng tôi chưa?

sentences.speaker: Hannah
sentences.text: Yes, I came for a check-up last year.
sentences.translationVi: Có, tôi đã đến khám tổng quát vào năm ngoái.

sentences.speaker: Hannah
sentences.text: My name is Hannah Lee.
sentences.translationVi: Tên tôi là Hannah Lee.

sentences.speaker: Robert
sentences.text: Thank you.
sentences.translationVi: Cảm ơn bạn.

sentences.speaker: Robert
sentences.text: What seems to be the problem with your tooth?
sentences.translationVi: Răng của bạn có vấn đề gì vậy?

sentences.speaker: Hannah
sentences.text: One of my back teeth hurts when I drink something cold.
sentences.translationVi: Một trong những chiếc răng hàm của tôi bị đau khi tôi uống đồ lạnh.

sentences.speaker: Robert
sentences.text: That could be a small cavity.
sentences.translationVi: Đó có thể là một lỗ sâu nhỏ.

sentences.speaker: Robert
sentences.text: The dentist will take an X-ray first to be sure.
sentences.translationVi: Nha sĩ sẽ chụp X-quang trước để chắc chắn.

sentences.speaker: Hannah
sentences.text: I see.
sentences.translationVi: Tôi hiểu rồi.

sentences.speaker: Hannah
sentences.text: How much does a filling usually cost?
sentences.translationVi: Trám răng thường có giá bao nhiêu?

sentences.speaker: Robert
sentences.text: A simple filling is around ninety dollars, but it depends on the size.
sentences.translationVi: Một ca trám đơn giản khoảng chín mươi đô la, nhưng còn tùy vào kích thước.

sentences.speaker: Hannah
sentences.text: Does my insurance cover part of it?
sentences.translationVi: Bảo hiểm của tôi có chi trả một phần không?

sentences.speaker: Robert
sentences.text: It often does.
sentences.translationVi: Thường là có.

sentences.speaker: Robert
sentences.text: Please bring your insurance card so we can check.
sentences.translationVi: Vui lòng mang theo thẻ bảo hiểm để chúng tôi kiểm tra.

sentences.speaker: Hannah
sentences.text: Okay.
sentences.translationVi: Được rồi.

sentences.speaker: Hannah
sentences.text: Will the treatment hurt?
sentences.translationVi: Việc điều trị có đau không?

sentences.speaker: Robert
sentences.text: Don't worry.
sentences.translationVi: Đừng lo lắng.

sentences.speaker: Robert
sentences.text: The dentist will numb the area, so you won't feel much pain.
sentences.translationVi: Nha sĩ sẽ gây tê vùng đó, nên bạn sẽ không thấy đau nhiều.

sentences.speaker: Hannah
sentences.text: That's a relief.
sentences.translationVi: Thật nhẹ nhõm.

sentences.speaker: Hannah
sentences.text: Can I come in this Thursday morning?
sentences.translationVi: Tôi có thể đến vào sáng thứ Năm này không?

sentences.speaker: Robert
sentences.text: We have an opening at ten o'clock.
sentences.translationVi: Chúng tôi còn một chỗ trống lúc mười giờ.

sentences.speaker: Robert
sentences.text: I'll book you in for then.
sentences.translationVi: Tôi sẽ đặt lịch cho bạn vào lúc đó.

sentences.speaker: Hannah
sentences.text: Perfect.
sentences.translationVi: Tuyệt vời.

sentences.speaker: Hannah
sentences.text: Thank you for your help.
sentences.translationVi: Cảm ơn bạn đã giúp đỡ.

sentences.speaker: Robert
sentences.text: You're welcome.
sentences.translationVi: Không có gì.

sentences.speaker: Robert
sentences.text: We'll see you on Thursday at ten.
sentences.translationVi: Hẹn gặp bạn vào thứ Năm lúc mười giờ.

Yes, I came for a check-up last year.

A simple filling is around ninety dollars, but it depends on the size.

grammarNotes.title: Predictions and offers with 'will'
grammarNotes.bodyVi: Dùng 'will + động từ' để nói về điều sẽ xảy ra hoặc đề nghị làm gì. Ví dụ: 'The dentist will take an X-ray' và 'I'll book you in for then.'
grammarNotes.bodyEn: Use 'will + verb' to talk about what is going to happen or to offer to do something. Examples: 'The dentist will take an X-ray' and 'I'll book you in for then.'

grammarNotes.title: Result clauses with 'so'
grammarNotes.bodyVi: Dùng 'so + mệnh đề' để nói về kết quả. Ví dụ: 'The dentist will numb the area, so you won't feel much pain.'
grammarNotes.bodyEn: Use 'so + clause' to express a result. Example: 'The dentist will numb the area, so you won't feel much pain.'

criticalThinkingQuestion: Why do you think many people feel nervous about going to the dentist, and what could help them feel more comfortable?
